sections.paragraph:
ὄναρ, τό (Hom. et al.; Herodas 1, 11; Pherecyd. 24; ins; Philo; Jos., Bell. 2, 112, Ant. 2, 63; 10, 195; Ath. 13, 1 οὐδʼ ὄναρ, but only in nom. and acc. sing.) dream, in our lit. only Mt chapters 1, 2, 27, and in the expr. κατʼ ὄναρ in a dream (rejected by Photius, Lex. p. 149, 25f as a barbarism [Lob., Phryn. p. 422ff], but attested fr. the time of Conon [I b.c./I a.d.]: 26 Fgm. 1, 35, 3 Jac., Apollo gives orders; Strabo 4, 1, 4; Anth. Pal. 11, 263, 1; Diog. L. 10, 32; Eunap. 55; CIG 4331 χρηματισθεὶς κατὰ ὄναρ=‘after an oracle had been given me in a dream’ [sim. of Sarapis IG XI/4, 13f ὁ θεός μοι ἐχρημάτισεν κατὰ τὸν ὕπνον]. Also SIG 1147; 1148/9; IPergamon 357, 8 [Schweizer 157]; IG XII/1, 979, 4f, but here means ‘as a result of a dream’ [Dssm., NB 81=BS 253], as Paus. Attic. λ, 28.—S. OWeinreich, θεοὶ ἐπήκοοι MAI 37, 1912, 43, n. 1) Mt 1:20; 2:12f, 19, 22; 27:19.—EEhrlich, D. Traum im AT ’53, D. Traum im Talmud: ZNW 47, ’56, 133–45; AWikenhauser, Pisciculi=Antike u. Christentum, Erg.-Bd. I ’39, 320–33; other lit. OSchönberger, Longos4 ’60, 178 n. on Longus 7, 1. On incubation oracles s. JGriffiths, Apuleius of Madauros 75, 139; EDodds, The Greeks and the Irrational ’51, 107–11.—B. 269. New Docs 1, 31; 3, 23. DELG. M-M. TW.

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Herodas Herodas , a.k.a. Herondas, III b.c.—List 5

Pherecyd. Pherecyd. = Pherecydes of Syros, VI b.c.—List 5

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Ath. Ath. = Athenagoras, II a.d.—List 5

acc. acc. = accusative

sing. sing. = singular

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

expr. expr. = expression

Lob. Lob. = CLobeck, s. Phryn.—List 5

Phryn Phryn , the Atticist, signifying his Eclogae, II a.d.—List 5

  1. fr. = from

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Jac. Jac. = Die Fragmente der griechischen Historiker, ed. FJacoby—Lists 5, 6

Strabo Strabo , I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Diog. L. Diog. L. = Diogenes Laertius, III a.d.—List 5

Eunap Eunap , IV–V a.d.—List 5

CIG CIG = Corpus Inscriptionum Graecarum—List 3

sim. sim. = similarly

IG IG = Inscriptiones Graecae—List 3

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

IPergamon IPergamon =Die Inschriften von Pergamon—List 3

Schweizer Schweizer = ES., Grammatik der pergamenischen Inschriften—Lists 3, 6

Dssm. Dssm. = ADeissmann, various works—List 6

BS BS , s. Dssm., BS—List 6

Paus Paus , perieg., II a.d.—List 5

MAI MAI = Mitteilungen des Deutschen Archäologischen Instituts—Lists 3, 6

ZNW ZNW = Zeitschrift für die ntl. Wissenschaft—List 6

Longus Longus , II ?a.d.—List 5

Apuleius Apuleius , wr. in Latin, II a.d.—List 5

  1. B. = CDBuck, A Dictionary of Selected Synonyms—List 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 710
